software operated / projects handled or involved with * company's training manual and maintenance manual aircraft design experience the aircraft design is a two-seater fixed tricycle type (nose wheel type) that was designed for flight training, touring and personal use. our plane utilizes basic flight controls that can help student pilots to have more control over the aircraft. the aircraft weights 1,500 and has a four-cylinder air-cooled horizontally opposed piston engine (continental o-200) that has a power loading of 15 to 17 ib/hp. its wing is a straight tapered platform low wing position one of the advantages of the aircraft design that we made compared to other aircraft is its wing is a straight tapered platform low wing position that can help pilots to see on what's above and below them clearly. research study experience the ramifications of the halting of operations on the majority of the economic and social sectors, particularly the aviation industry, during the covid-19 pandemic have caused the acceleration of increasing interest in unmanned aerial vehicles (uav) around the world. the rapidly growing concern of limiting contact in conducting operations in order to limit the exposure to the virus along with the assurance of the continuously expanding technological environment has prompted the consideration of integrating uav operations routinely for cargo and passenger transport operations. this study entitled perceptions of aeronautical engineering students of holy angel university towards next generation use of unmanned aerial vehicles for cargo delivery came up with the idea of using uav for delivering goods instead of using man power. this study generally aims to ascertain the perceptions of the aeronautical engineering students of holy angel university in such areas as their level of awareness and evaluate the insights, opinions, and reactions to the routine use of unmanned aircraft vehicles on cargo delivery services. computer
